rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [4+1] cycloaddition of arylidene/alkylidene chromane-2,4-diones with NaI as mediator/reactant to form spiro[benzofuran-2,2'-furan]-3-ones
electrochemical_role: Cathode reduces the α,β-unsaturated chromane-2,4-dione 1a to a radical anion (1a'); iodide is oxidized at the anode to I2 which iodinates the substrate; cathode also reduces water generating hydroxide that hydrolyzes/opens the ring; the final iodo-α-elimination yields a carbene that undergoes [4+1] with another molecule of 1a.

key_intermediates.name: α-iodo benzofuran-3(2H)-one 1c' (after decarboxylation and iodination of enolate)
benzofuran-3(2H)-one-derived carbene 1c'' (after PhCHO release and α-elimination of I-)

electron_flow: Cathode injects an electron into 1a giving radical anion 1a', which combines with anodically generated I2 to form α-iodo radical 1a''; further anodic oxidation plus hydroxide addition gives 1a''', which ring-opens with water to 1b. Base-promoted cyclization, decarboxylation, and a second iodination give 1c', then loss of PhCHO with α-elimination of iodide generates carbene 1c'' that undergoes intermolecular [4+1] cycloaddition with 1a to give spirocycle 2a, while cathodic water reduction produces H2 and OH- (paired electrolysis).

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed (Table 1, entry 30), supporting a radical intermediate

notes: X-ray structure (CCDC 2262815) of product 2j confirms the spirocyclic connectivity and diastereochemistry. GC-MS detection of free benzaldehyde supports the proposed extrusion step from 1c' en route to carbene 1c''. CV shows a reduction peak of 1a near -1.28 V vs Ag/AgCl distinct from solvent; 1a is not anodically oxidizable, consistent with cathodic initiation. Gram-scale electrolysis (67%) demonstrates scalability.
